name: 00.03.create-agents-md-file

agent: agent
model: GPT-5.6 Luna (copilot)
---

# Analyse repository content and create an AGENTS.md file in the root directory

Recursively and methodically analyse this repository content and, based on its context, create an appropriate `AGENTS.md` file in the repository root.

## Schema definition

For the schema, use the 10 most widely adopted sections for initial review and further iteration.

## Anticipated output

The prompt fixes the section count at ten but does not rely on an official
standardized `AGENTS.md` schema. Use the following editorial pattern, derived
from common repository, contributor, and coding-agent guidance. Tailor every
section to evidence found in the repository instead of copying generic text.

| Section                                 | Anticipated repository evidence and purpose                                                       |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Repository Overview                     | Establish the repository's purpose, audience, and important context.                              |
| Repository Structure                    | Identify active source areas, reference material, generated files, and ownership boundaries.      |
| Prerequisites And Setup                 | Record required SDKs, runtimes, services, and initial setup commands.                             |
| Build And Run                           | Provide authoritative commands for restoring, building, and starting the applications.            |
| Testing                                 | Describe test frameworks, dependencies, focused checks, and full validation commands.             |
| Architecture And Boundaries             | Explain component responsibilities and dependencies that must remain separated.                   |
| Coding And Documentation Conventions    | Consolidate applicable language, naming, formatting, and scoped-instruction rules.                |
| Security And Configuration              | Define credential handling, input validation, secure defaults, and sensitive configuration rules. |
| Automation And Deployment               | Cover CI/CD, infrastructure, generated assets, and deployment approval boundaries.                |
| Change Workflow And Completion Criteria | Define safe editing, required validation, diff review, and completion reporting.                  |

Use these exact level-two headings in the root `AGENTS.md` for the initial
version:

```markdown
## Repository Overview

## Repository Structure

## Prerequisites And Setup

## Build And Run

## Testing

## Architecture And Boundaries

## Coding And Documentation Conventions

## Security And Configuration

## Automation And Deployment

## Change Workflow And Completion Criteria
```

The pattern forms four practical groups:

- Orientation: overview and structure
- Execution: prerequisites, build, and testing
- Engineering constraints: architecture, conventions, and security
- Delivery: automation and completion workflow

Before writing, recursively inspect the live repository and distinguish active implementation paths from historical, completed, generated, or reference
content. Include only commands, tools, architecture, and constraints supported by repository evidence. Link to detailed instructions instead of duplicating
them, and report any category that does not apply rather than inventing content.
